text:
Human Capital Migration and the Sustainable Development of Socio-Economic Systems Evolutionary dynamics of migration and its impact on human capital Migration has a twofold, evolutionary character: on the one hand, the influx of foreigners leads to difficulties in social protection systems; on the other hand, countries with a high level of immigration have a higher degree of socio-economic sustainability and at the same time become key competitors for highly qualified personnel.

The study provides an in-depth examination of quantitative and qualitative characteristics of population movements that determine the main factors of internal and international migration of EU countries and Ukraine, migration intensity and level of emigration and forms the socio-demographic characteristics of migrants before and after the full-scale invasion in Ukraine. In addition, the study assesses and characterizes the threats, losses and opportunities of human capital migration and consequences for achieving SDGs in donor and recipient countries, and on the basis of it presents a new research agenda for adjusting the policy of regulation of population movement to the requirements of sustainable development.
